description: Magic of misdirection and false reality — conjuring phantom images, weaving mirages, and cloaking truth in shadow.
abilities: Imagining a creature and conjuring a convincing illusion of it nearby that looks, moves, sounds, and even feels real to the touch. The illusion can sustain some damage before vanishing and may behave independently, traveling away from the caster on programmed routines such as cleaning, patrolling, or guarding a door. Rules can be set for its behavior, and the caster shares a telepathic bond with the illusion when nearby, able to control it directly, make it move, act, speak, and even wield weapons for basic attacks. After a day, the illusion wanders out of sight and vanishes. At greater power, the spell can create a perfect double of an existing creature, fooling everyone except the original's closest friends and family.

Rendering a nearby creature or object invisible for a brief time. If the target is a creature, everything worn and carried also vanishes from sight. Attacking while invisible breaks the spell. At greater power, multiple creatures or objects can be made invisible simultaneously, with the effort scaling for each additional target.

Summoning an illusory bird somewhere nearby to keep watch over an area within its sight, remaining until dismissed. Only one Little Bird may exist at a time. The caster shares a telepathic link with the bird, which sends a mental signal when it witnesses something specified, such as a creature entering a room or a door opening, and will even rouse the caster from sleep to report.

Producing tiny magical effects to surprise, delight, or confuse those nearby, choosing freely among them: a harmless display of light such as a flickering flame or sparking pattern, with the ability to snuff out or ignite small light sources; a brief sound effect like a wind chime, applause, or a whisper; a conjured smell of any kind, from freshly baked pie to a cesspool; or a gentle physical sensation felt by nearby creatures, like a tap on the shoulder, a chill breeze, or the prickle of goosebumps.

Conjuring a major illusion spanning a vast area. The illusion can depict anything that fits within the space, such as an oasis, a pile of treasure, or a lumbering giant guarding its territory, and may be programmed with looping behaviors. If a creature touches or passes through the illusion, they see through it and it vanishes for them. Creatures with the ability to detect magic may also discover the deception.

Summoning a small illusory servant that hovers over the caster's shoulder for the remainder of the outing, appearing as a blooming flower with an eye at its center, invisible to all except those the caster designates. The Monitor can be deployed just beyond the caster's line of sight, scouting around corners and into adjacent rooms, reporting how many creatures occupy a space and providing a rudimentary mental map of the area's size, shape, and major features.

Reading the mind of a nearby creature and producing an illusion of something it finds extremely irritating, visible and audible only to the caster and the target. For several minutes, the illusion relentlessly mocks and taunts the target, commanding its full attention. The caster may control the illusion directly, having it manipulate small objects, steal items, or lead the target on a chase, or simply let it run wild with its own mischievous behavior.

Vanishing momentarily and reappearing flanked by two illusory duplicates that perfectly mimic every movement. When a creature attempts to target the caster, there is an equal chance of striking a duplicate instead. A duplicate that takes any damage disappears.
